title: The Orion Nebula in Infrared
explanation: The great stellar nursery in Orion glows with the light of newly formed stars in this infrared composite from the Spitzer Space Telescope. Embedded protostars heat surrounding dust clouds, which radiate warmly across multiple infrared wavelengths.

title: Earth Rise Over the Lunar Limb
explanation: Taken from lunar orbit aboard the Artemis II crew capsule, this photograph shows our blue-and-white home world cresting the gray lunar horizon. The familiar continents of North and Central America are visible under scattered cloud cover.

title: The Milky Way Over the Mississippi Delta
explanation: On a moonless spring night a photographer standing in the Louisiana marshes captured the galactic core rising above the wetlands. The bright central bulge stretches from horizon to zenith while the dark Rift splits the band of stars nearly in two.

title: A Coronal Mass Ejection Erupts
explanation: The Solar Dynamics Observatory caught this dramatic solar flare and accompanying coronal mass ejection launching billions of tons of magnetized plasma into space. The eruption from active region AR3981 reached speeds exceeding 1,200 km/s.

title: Saturn's Rings in Exquisite Detail
explanation: Cassini data combined with new processing algorithms reveals the intricate wave patterns and shepherd moons embedded within Saturn's ring system. The Cassini Division and the delicate Encke Gap are sharply defined in this reprocessed mosaic.

title: Comet Pons-Brooks Near Perihelion
explanation: With its distinctive green coma and faint ion tail streaming away from the Sun, Comet 12P/Pons-Brooks put on a memorable spring show. Imaged from New Mexico at 40 minutes of exposure, the comet's nucleus outburst had broadened its horseshoe-shaped coma.

title: The Crab Nebula Pulsar Wind Nebula
explanation: The Crab Nebula, the remnant of a supernova observed by Chinese astronomers in 1054 AD, continues to expand at roughly 1,500 km/s. At its heart a pulsar spinning 30 times per second energizes the surrounding web of blue synchrotron filaments visible here in a Hubble composite.

title: Jupiter's Moon Io in Volcanic Eruption
explanation: A JunoCam perijove pass captured towering plumes from the volcano Loki Patera on Jupiter's moon Io. The umbrella-shaped eruption column reached an estimated 400 kilometers above the sulfur-coated surface, glowing orange against the black of space.

title: A Moonbow Over the Smoky Mountains
explanation: A rare lunar rainbow arches over a misty Tennessee valley in this long-exposure photograph taken near the spring equinox. Moonbows form the same way as solar rainbows but require a bright full moon opposite the observer from falling rain.

title: The Eagle Nebula's Pillars of Creation Revisited
explanation: The James Webb Space Telescope's MIRI instrument adds a new dimension to this iconic star-forming region in Serpens. Mid-infrared light pierces the dusty pillars to reveal dozens of embedded young stellar objects still gathering mass from their natal clouds.

title: Annular Solar Eclipse Path Over the Pacific
explanation: A composite of 23 images shot from a research vessel in the South Pacific captures the ring-of-fire eclipse as the Moon's antumbra crossed open ocean. The reddish chromosphere of the Sun is briefly visible just inside the dark lunar disk.

title: Aurora Australis From the International Space Station
explanation: Expedition 72 crew members photographed shimmering green and crimson curtains of aurora australis sweeping over the Southern Ocean. The ISS was traveling at roughly 7.7 km/s while the auroral display rippled in response to an ongoing geomagnetic storm.

title: The Triangulum Galaxy in 665 Megapixels
explanation: A mosaic assembled from 54 Hubble pointings delivers the most detailed view yet of M33, the Triangulum Galaxy. Individual blue supergiant stars, red giant star clusters, and pink emission nebulae are resolved across the galaxy's 60,000-light-year disk.

title: Tour of the Carina Nebula
explanation: This visualization constructed from Webb infrared data flies through the towering gas-and-dust peaks of the Carina Nebula, home to some of the most massive and luminous stars in the Milky Way. The three-minute journey takes the viewer from the outer wisps to the cavity carved by Eta Carinae.

title: Perseid Meteors Over the Gulf Coast
explanation: A composite of 47 individual frames recorded from a dark beach near Gulf Shores, Alabama, maps Perseid meteors radiating from their namesake constellation. The bright Milky Way arches behind the display, and a single bright fireball leaves a glowing train visible for nearly four seconds.

title: Titan's Haze Layers From Cassini
explanation: Cassini's narrow-angle camera recorded at least seven distinct haze layers wrapping Saturn's moon Titan as the spacecraft passed through the moon's shadow. Photochemical smog produced by sunlight breaking apart nitrogen and methane molecules builds these stratified layers.

title: The Lunar South Pole From LRO
explanation: The Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter's wide-angle camera mapped this permanently shadowed region near Shackleton Crater at the Moon's south pole. These cold traps preserve water ice deposited over billions of years by comets and asteroids, making them prime candidates for future exploration.

title: The Veil Nebula in Hydrogen and Oxygen
explanation: A delicate web of glowing gas marks the expanding remnant of a supernova in Cygnus. The Veil Nebula is about 2,100 light-years away and spans roughly 110 light-years across, the visible portion representing only a fraction of the full Cygnus Loop remnant.

title: A Total Lunar Eclipse over the Andes
explanation: The fully eclipsed Moon glows copper-red above a high desert ridge line near San Pedro de Atacama, Chile. During totality the Moon passed through the deep umbra of Earth's shadow, lit only by sunlight refracted and reddened by all of Earth's sunrises and sunsets simultaneously.

title: Jupiter and Its Great Red Spot
explanation: A sharpened amateur image using lucky-imaging techniques reveals swirling bands and the famous centuries-old storm system known as the Great Red Spot. The anticyclone is currently about 1.3 times the diameter of Earth and continues a slow multi-decade shrinkage.

title: Noctilucent Clouds at Midnight
explanation: Electric-blue clouds at the edge of space shine after sunset over the Baltic Sea in Sweden. Noctilucent clouds form in the mesosphere at altitudes around 80 km when water vapor freezes onto meteor smoke particles, becoming visible only when illuminated by sunlight while the lower atmosphere is in darkness.

title: The Andromeda Galaxy Up Close
explanation: A mosaic of our nearest large galactic neighbor spanning six degrees of sky was assembled from 411 individual frames. At 2.5 million light-years the Andromeda Galaxy is the most distant object visible to the unaided eye, and contains roughly one trillion stars.

title: A Flight Through the Orion Nebula
explanation: A short visualization soars through the glowing star-forming region of the Orion Nebula, constructed from Hubble and Webb imagery. The flight begins at the outer shell of ionized gas and dives past the Trapezium cluster before banking through the dark BN-KL complex.

title: Saturn at Opposition
explanation: Rings tilted toward Earth catch the sunlight at the planet's closest approach to Earth during its annual opposition. The Cassini Division is clearly visible in this backyard telescope image, along with the faint shadow the planet casts across the ring plane.

title: Sunspot Region AR4012 in Close Up
explanation: A high-resolution view of a complex sunspot group near the solar limb was captured by the GREGOR solar telescope in the Canary Islands. The sunspot's umbra and penumbra are surrounded by granulation cells roughly 1,000 km across representing convection at the solar surface.

title: The Helix Nebula: A Cosmic Eye
explanation: Often called the Eye of God, the Helix Nebula in Aquarius is one of the nearest planetary nebulae to Earth at just 650 light-years distance. This Hubble composite shows concentric rings of glowing gas shed by a dying sun-like star over thousands of years.

title: Mars at Its Closest: Syrtis Major in Detail
explanation: The dark volcanic plateau of Syrtis Major stands out in this amateur image taken during Mars's closest approach. The bright Hellas Basin, one of the largest impact craters in the solar system, glows with dust to the south while the north polar ice cap is visible at the top.

title: The Butterfly Nebula in Narrow-Band Light
explanation: The bipolar planetary nebula NGC 6302 in Scorpius, nicknamed the Butterfly Nebula, expands at over 600,000 km/h from one of the hottest known white dwarfs at its center. Hydrogen, sulfur, and oxygen emission combined in this Hubble palette image creates the vivid gold and blue wings.

title: The Summer Solstice Sunrise at Stonehenge
explanation: Thousands gathered at Stonehenge to watch the midsummer sunrise align with the Heel Stone as it has done for roughly 4,500 years. This telephoto shot from 400 meters away compresses the scene so the disk of the rising Sun appears to balance precisely on the ancient megalith.

title: The Lagoon Nebula's Central Hourglass
explanation: The Lagoon Nebula in Sagittarius is a stellar nursery 4,100 light-years away and one of the finest emission nebulae visible to the naked eye from southern latitudes. This close-up from Webb's NIRCam resolves the turbulent Hourglass region where the young star Herschel 36 sculpts surrounding gas.
